title: myrtus coriacea
intro: Myrtus coriacea, also known as the 'lemon myrtle' or 'brown myrtle', is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its aromatic leaves with a citrusy flavor and fragrance. The medicinal properties of Myrtus coriacea are attributed to its essential oil, which contains compounds like citral and limonene, exhibiting antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to overall well-being. Myrtus coriacea is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and flavorings, as well as in aromatherapy applications to promote relaxation and alleviate stress.
